Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiff Alexandra Elchinoff To Appear For An Independent Mental Examination
Off Calendar Mar-02-2016 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances in the matter. DEFENDANT ELSA YEE, STEVE MENDOZA-YEE Motion To Compel Plaintiff Alexandra Elchinoff To Appear For An Independent Mental Examination is off calendar. Duplicate motion. Ruling issued on March 1, 2016. =(302/HK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiff Alexandra Elchinoff To Appear For Independent Mental Examination
Off Calendar Mar-01-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS ELSA YEE AND STEVE MENDOZA-YEE'S Motion To Compel Plaintiff Alexandra Elchinoff To Appear For Independent Mental Examination - Defendant's request for a CCP 2032.320 mental examination is granted. Plaintiff's mental state is clearly at issue in the litigation. The limitation requested by plaintiff that both the interview and psychological testing not exceed six hours is granted. Absent a declaration from the examining physician there is no basis for an extension of examining time to nine hours. The psychological tests to be administered are the three tests identified in defendant's notice of motion. The questioner may inquire into plaintiff's substance abuse history as that information is potentially relevant to her mental condition and claim. Inquiry into plaintiff's recreational activities may be relevant and does not significantly invade her privacy so as to warrant restricting discussion on that topic. Inquiry by the examiner into plaintiff's "legal history" is in all respects denied. The connection between her legal history and claim in the instant action is remote and the danger of questioning of the plaintiff extending into matters protected by the attorney-client privilege is obvious. The request for a protective order is granted. The entirety of the mental examination shall be held in strict confidence. The parties shall meet and confer prior to the issuance the examiner's report as to the language of said Order which the Court shall sign. The examination shall be held as presently scheduled by notice on March 3, 2016. The prevailing party is to provide a form of order that tracks the tentative ruling by way of e mail to the Pro Tem Judge. Opposing parties must be noticed in the e mail.

Judge Pro Tem: Steven Stein. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Not reported. (302/JPT)
